zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Emma Bingham於1409年出生在英國諾福爾可賓納姆時，她的父親Rafe Bingham30歲，母親Elizabeth26歲。她於1429年與John Marshall在英國英格蘭諾福爾可辛加姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1457年8月8日在英國越可爾克雷文享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾可拉法姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Emma Bingham于1409年出生在英国諾福爾可賓納姆时，她的父亲Rafe Bingham30岁，母亲Elizabeth26岁。她于1429年与John Marshall在英国英格兰諾福爾可辛加姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1457年8月8日在英国越可爾克雷文享年48岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾可拉法姆。

nameMeanings.name: Bingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Dorset) and Irish (County Mayo): habitational name from Bingham (Nottinghamshire). The placename is probably from an Old English folk-name Bynningas (‘the people associated with a man named Bynna’), or possibly from an unattested Old English word bing ‘a kettle-shaped hollow’, + Old English hām ‘homestead’.
Irish (Ulster, of Scottish origin): altered form of   Bigham  .
American shortened and altered form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames such as   Bingenheimer  .

variants.name: Ingham
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of the places called Ingham, particularly in Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Suffolk. The placename is from the Old English personal name Inga + hām ‘homestead’. In Yorkshire this name was sometimes confused with Bingham in the 18th century.
English (of Norman origin): variant of the obsolete Norman surname Engaine; see Gain .

variants.name: Bigham
meanings: Scottish (Wigtownshire) and Irish: habitational name from either Bigholm in Beith parish (Ayrshire) or Bigholms in Langholm parish (Dumfriesshire). The second element of the placename is from Older Scots holm (Old Norse holmr) ‘islet’, ‘raised land in a marsh’, ‘a stretch of low-lying land beside a river; a meadow’. The first element may be an unattested Older Scots bigge, a shortened form of bigging ‘building’. The name was taken to Ulster in the 17th and 18th centuries, where it was used interchangeably with Bingham .

variants.name: Bingenheimer
meanings: German and Jewish (from Germany): habitational name for someone from Bingenheim on the Eder river (Hesse). Compare   Bingham  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Emma Bingham was born in 1409, in Binham, Norfolk, England, her father, Rafe Bingham, was 30 and her mother, Elizabeth, was 26. She married John Marshall in 1429, in Hingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 8 August 1457, in Craven, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 48, and was buried in Clapham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Emma Bingham est née en 1409, à Binham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Rafe Bingham, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé John Marshall en 1429, à Hingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 8 août 1457, à Craven, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Clapham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Emma Bingham 1409 in Binham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Rafe Bingham 30 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth 26. Sie hat 1429 in Hingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom John Marshall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 8. August 1457 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Craven, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Clapham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Emma Bingham è nata il nel 1409 nel Binham, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Rafe Bingham, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato John Marshall nel 1429, nel Hingham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 agosto 1457, nel Craven, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Clapham, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Emma Binghamが1409年にイギリスノーフォルクビンハムで生まれた時，父Rafe Binghamは30歳，母Elizabethは26歳であった。1429年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヒンガムでJohn Marshallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1457年8月8日にイギリスヨークシュアイアクラベンで48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアクラファムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Emma Bingham(이)가 1409년 영국 노르폴크 빈함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rafe Bingham(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 잉함에서 John Marshall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 8월 8일 영국 요르크실르 크레이븐에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클라프아에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Emma Bingham nasceu em 1409, em Binham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Rafe Bingham, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com John Marshall em 1429, em Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 8 de agosto de 1457, em Craven, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Clapham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Emma Bingham родилась в 1409 г. в местности бинам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Rafe Bingham было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Marshall в 1429 г. в местности хингхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 8 августа 1457 г. в местности кравен, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности клафам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Emma Bingham nació en 1409, en Binham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Rafe Bingham, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 26 años. Se casó con John Marshall en 1429, en Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de agosto de 1457, en Craven, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Clapham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
